subject: Android Keystore RSA with ECB block mode and PKCS1Padding
truth: Stop using RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding which defaults to insecure ECB mode; use RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-256AndMGF1Padding or similar.

subject: WebView allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs
truth: Stop enabling allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs; it relaxes CORS so file:// pages can access content:// URIs, enabling data exfiltration via XSS.
quote: allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs is required in this attack since it relaxes the default restrictions so that pages loaded from file:// can access content from any origin (including content:// URIs)

subject: ContentProvider query returning all rows without permission check
truth: Stop returning all database rows from query() without verifying caller permissions; enforce android:readPermission on the provider.
quote: any app on the device can read patient records via: content://org.owasp.mastestapp.appointments/appointments

subject: iOS random token generation using drand48 linear congruential generator
truth: Stop using drand48 for random tokens; use SecRandomCopyBytes instead.
quote: Insecure: drand48 uses a 48 bit linear congruential generator. Not thread safe and not suitable for cryptographic purposes

subject: drand48() linear congruential generator in iOS Swift
truth: Stop using drand48() for any security-relevant random values; use SecRandomCopyBytes instead.
quote: Insecure: drand48 uses a 48 bit linear congruential generator. Not thread safe and not suitable for cryptographic purposes

subject: Custom URL Scheme Source Validation on iOS
truth: Stop relying solely on sourceApplication for URL scheme validation; Apple only populates it for apps in the same Developer Team, so cross-team/system apps always return nil.
quote: Apple only populates sourceApplication when the calling app belongs to the same Apple Developer Team. Apps from other teams or system apps (e.g. Notes, Safari) will have sourceApplication set to nil.

subject: Missing origin validation in WKScriptMessageHandler
truth: Always validate the message origin in WKScriptMessageHandler before processing sensitive actions; the handler receives messages from any JavaScript in the WebView regardless of page origin.
quote: exposes sensitive native data to any JavaScript running in the WebView without validating the origin or the content of the incoming message

subject: iOS fprintf logging sensitive data
truth: Stop using fprintf for logging sensitive data; remove or guard all fprintf calls behind DEBUG conditionals.

subject: iOS NSAssert/NSCAssert leaking sensitive data
truth: Stop using NSAssert and NSCAssert with sensitive data in production; assertions remain in release builds and can leak data via crash logs.
